records.ety: [L. nobilitas: cf. OF. nobilit. See Noble.]

defs: The quality or state of being noble; superiority of mind or of character; commanding excellence; eminence.
quotes: Though she hated Amphialus, yet the nobility of her courage prevailed over it.
They thought it great their sovereign to control, And named their pride nobility of soul.

defs: The state of being of high rank or noble birth; patrician dignity; antiquity of family; distinction by rank, station, or title, whether inherited or conferred.
quotes: I fell on the same argument of preferring virtue to nobility of blood and titles, in the story of Sigismunda.

defs: Those who are noble; the collective body of nobles or titled persons in a state; the aristocratic and patrician class; the peerage; as, the English nobility.
